stem: What is the sum of the interior angles of a pentagon (5 sides)?
solutionStandard: \((5-2) \times 180 = 3 \times 180 = 540\) degrees.

stem: What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular hexagon (6 sides)?
solutionStandard: Sum of interior angles: \((6-2)\times180=720\). Each angle: \(720/6=120\) degrees.

stem: A regular polygon has an exterior angle of 40° at each vertex. How many sides does it have?
solutionStandard: Exterior angles sum to 360°: \(360/40=9\) sides.

stem: Compare the two quantities.
quantityA: The sum of interior angles of a hexagon
quantityB: The sum of interior angles of two triangles
solutionStandard: Hexagon: \((6-2)\times180=720\). Two triangles: \(2\times180=360\). Since \(720>360\), Quantity A is greater.

stem: A polygon has an interior angle sum of 1440°. How many sides does it have?
A regular polygon has interior angles of 150° each. How many sides does it have?
solutionStandard: Each exterior angle is \(180-150=30\) degrees. Number of sides: \(360/30=12\).

stem: Which of the following are true for every convex polygon? Select all that apply.

options.text: The interior angle sum depends on the number of sides
Each interior angle is less than 180°

solutionStandard: (A) is always true (exterior angles always sum to 360°). (B) is only true for quadrilaterals specifically, not all polygons. (C) is true — the sum is \((n-2)\times180\). (D) is part of the definition of convex.

stem: In a regular polygon, each interior angle is 8 times its corresponding exterior angle. How many sides does the polygon have?
solutionStandard: Let the exterior angle be \(x\); the interior angle is \(8x\). Since they're supplementary: \(x+8x=180\), so \(x=20\). Number of sides: \(360/20=18\).

stem: A pentagon has four angles measuring 100°, 110°, 120°, and 95°. What is the measure of the fifth angle?
solutionStandard: Sum of interior angles of a pentagon: \((5-2)\times180=540\). Fifth angle: \(540-(100+110+120+95)=540-425=115\).

stem: Polygon A is a regular octagon (8 sides). Polygon B is a regular decagon (10 sides).
quantityA: Each interior angle of Polygon A
quantityB: Each interior angle of Polygon B
solutionStandard: Octagon: \((8-2)\times180/8=135\). Decagon: \((10-2)\times180/10=144\). Since \(144>135\), Quantity B is greater. (In general, more sides means each interior angle of a regular polygon gets closer to 180°.)

stem: What is the sum of the interior angles of a polygon with {{n}} sides?
What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular octagon (8 sides)?
solutionStandard: Sum of interior angles: \((8-2)\times180=1080\). Each angle: \(1080/8=135\) degrees.

stem: A polygon has an interior angle sum of {{(nt-2)*180}}°. How many sides does it have?
A polygon has {{n}} sides.
quantityA: The sum of interior angles of the polygon
quantityB: The sum of interior angles of {{n-3}} triangles
solutionStandard: The polygon's angle sum is \(({{n}}-2)\times180\). \({{n}}-3\) triangles sum to \(({{n}}-3)\times180\), which is always \(180^\circ\) less than the polygon's sum. Quantity A is greater.

stem: A regular polygon has interior angles of {{180 - 360/nt}}° each. How many sides does it have?
solutionStandard: Each exterior angle is \(360/{{nt}}={{360/nt}}\) degrees. Number of sides: \(360/{{360/nt}}={{nt}}\).

stem: Which of the following are true for every regular polygon? Select all that apply.

options.text: All sides are equal in length.
It has an even number of sides.

solutionStandard: Equal side lengths and equal angles are part of the definition of "regular," and regular polygons are always convex. (D) is false — a regular pentagon has 5 (odd) sides.

stem: A pentagon has four angles measuring 95°, 105°, 115°, and 100°. What is the measure of the fifth angle?
solutionStandard: Sum of interior angles of a pentagon: \((5-2)\times180=540\). Fifth angle: \(540-(95+105+115+100)=540-415=125\).

stem: In a regular polygon, each interior angle is 5 times its corresponding exterior angle. How many sides does the polygon have?
solutionStandard: Let the exterior angle be \(x\); the interior angle is \(5x\). Since they're supplementary: \(x+5x=180\), so \(x=30\). Number of sides: \(360/30=12\).

stem: A hexagon has five angles summing to {{720 - mt}}°. What is the measure of the sixth angle?
solutionStandard: A hexagon's interior angles sum to \((6-2)\times180=720\). The sixth angle is \(720-{{720-mt}}={{mt}}\).

stem: Polygon A is a regular \(n\)-gon. Polygon B is a regular \((n+1)\)-gon, for some integer \(n \ge 3\).
quantityA: Each interior angle of Polygon A
quantityB: Each interior angle of Polygon B
solutionStandard: Each interior angle of a regular \(k\)-gon is \(\dfrac{(k-2)\times180}{k} = 180 - \dfrac{360}{k}\), which increases as \(k\) increases (since \(360/k\) shrinks). So a regular \((n+1)\)-gon always has a larger interior angle than a regular \(n\)-gon. Quantity B is greater.
